name: homeassistant-config
description: Create and manage Home Assistant YAML configuration files including automations, scripts, templates, blueprints, Lovelace dashboards, and file organization. Use when working with Home Assistant configuration files (.yaml, .yml) or discussing HA automations, scripts, sensors, or dashboards.
capabilities: Validate YAML syntax and Home Assistant structure
Build Lovelace dashboards with cards and views
author: ESJavadex
securitySummary: The static analyzer produced extensive false positives due to pattern-matching limitations when analyzing Home Assistant YAML configuration documentation. Key findings: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' (150+ occurrences) incorrectly flags YAML keywords like 'mode: restart' - not actual crypto operations. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' (448+ occurrences) incorrectly flags markdown code formatting backticks as command execution. 'System reconnaissance' flags documentation of file operations like 'find' and 'grep' commands. The Python validation scripts use standard file operations (read_text, os.walk, yaml.safe_load) for legitimate configuration validation. No malicious code, network calls, credential exfiltration, or actual dangerous patterns detected. This is a legitimate Home Assistant configuration management skill.
valueStatement: Home Assistant configuration files require precise YAML syntax and proper structure. This skill provides validation tools, example configurations, and best practices for automations, scripts, templates, blueprints, and Lovelace dashboards.
userTitle: Create Home Assistant automations and dashboards
limitations: Does not execute or deploy configurations to Home Assistant
Does not access Home Assistant API or entity states
Does not validate runtime integration compatibility
Scripts require Python and PyYAML installed

useCases.title: Create first automation
useCases.description: Set up motion-activated lights with proper YAML structure and syntax

useCases.title: Build complex dashboards
useCases.description: Design Lovelace dashboards with custom cards, conditional displays, and responsive layouts

useCases.title: Optimize configuration
useCases.description: Find duplicate automations, refactor scripts, and implement blueprints for reusability

promptTemplates.title: Basic automation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an automation that turns on the living room light when motion is detected after sunset, with a 5-minute delay before turning off

promptTemplates.title: Template sensor
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a template sensor that calculates the total power usage from multiple smart plugs and shows daily energy cost

promptTemplates.title: Lovelace dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Mushroom cards configuration for the living room that shows temperature, humidity, and light controls in a compact layout

promptTemplates.title: Blueprint creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a blueprint for motion-activated lighting with configurable motion sensor, target light, wait time, and day/night brightness settings

bestPractices: Always use 2 spaces for indentation, never tabs
Use plural form for triggers, conditions, and actions
Add unique IDs to automations for UI editing support
antiPatterns: Using tabs instead of spaces in YAML files
Unquoted strings that look like booleans
Hardcoding entity IDs instead of using variables

faq.question: What versions of Home Assistant are supported?
faq.answer: This skill uses 2024+ syntax with modern trigger, condition, and action formats. Most patterns work with 2022.4+.

faq.question: What are the validation script requirements?
faq.answer: Scripts require Python 3 and PyYAML installed. Run pip install pyyaml before using validation tools.

faq.question: Can I integrate with existing Home Assistant?
faq.answer: This skill generates configuration files. You must manually copy or deploy files to your Home Assistant instance.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Scripts only read files for validation. No data is sent externally or stored beyond the session.

faq.question: Why does the skill need filesystem access?
faq.answer: File access is required to validate YAML syntax, check for duplicates, and analyze configuration structure.

faq.question: How does this compare to Home Assistant Studio?
faq.answer: This skill provides Claude Code integration for generating and validating configurations. Studio is a web-based editor.
